commit.message:
feat: add notifications system data model, API, and client

Add extensible notification system with three new entities:
- NotificationType: registry of notification types with channel support flags
- NotificationPreference: per-user, per-type channel preferences (InSite/Email)
- Notification: individual notification records for inbox and audit trail

Includes:
- EF Core entities with DbContext configuration and SQL table definitions
- Seed data for 4 initial notification types (weekly-admin-digest, ban-expiring-soon, new-player-report, unclaimed-ban)
- DTOs and API interfaces in Abstractions.V1
- API controllers: NotificationTypes (GET), NotificationPreferences (GET/PUT), Notifications (GET/POST/PATCH)
- API client implementations with version selectors and DI registration
- Fake API clients for testing
- 13 unit tests covering all new endpoints

commit.message:
Claude/opencode hooks plugin support (#31)

* feat: add OpenCode plugin support

Adds a native TypeScript plugin (plugin/opencode.ts) that integrates
otel-hook with OpenCode's plugin system, following the same thin-wrapper
pattern as rtk.

- plugin/opencode.ts: exports OtelHookPlugin; checks otel-hook is in
  PATH at load time, then hooks session.created/deleted → SessionStart/
  SessionEnd and tool.execute.before/after → PreToolUse/PostToolUse,
  piping JSON payloads to otel-hook via stdin with IDE_OTEL_IDE_NAME=opencode

- setup.sh: adds --opencode flag and setup_opencode() function that
  copies the plugin to .opencode/plugins/ (project) or
  ~/.config/opencode/plugins/ (--global); auto-detects OpenCode via
  command -v opencode or ~/.config/opencode; respects OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR

- examples/opencode-plugin.example.ts: annotated copy for users who
  want to customise the plugin

- README.md: replaces the placeholder OpenCode blurb with a proper
  setup section including quick-start commands and manual install steps

- tests/test_otel_hook.py: adds TestOpenCodePluginPayloads (11 tests)
  covering IDE detection, event normalisation, and priority ordering for
  all four payload types emitted by the plugin

https://claude.ai/code/session_01HpMgSN8uCgNypFeuDCo7x3

* feat(opencode): handle all observable hook events

Extends plugin/opencode.ts (and the matching example) to cover the full
set of OpenCode events that map to canonical otel-hook events:

  session.idle    → Stop            (agent finished responding)
  message.updated → UserPromptSubmit (role=user messages only)
  session.error   → SessionEnd with status=error (closes trace on failure)

The previous commit only wired session.created/deleted and the tool
execute lifecycle. The three new branches follow the same thin-wrapper
pattern: build a minimal JSON payload and pipe it to otel-hook via
IDE_OTEL_IDE_NAME=opencode.

Tests: adds 8 new assertions to TestOpenCodePluginPayloads covering
IDE detection and event-name extraction for every payload shape the
updated plugin emits. All 185 tests pass.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01HpMgSN8uCgNypFeuDCo7x3

* docs: update Supported Events table with OpenCode plugin column

- Splits "Claude Code / Antigravity / compatible runners" into two
  separate columns: one for Claude Code / Antigravity, one for
  OpenCode (plugin) showing the native OpenCode event names that
  each canonical event maps from (session.created, session.idle,
  message.updated, tool.execute.before/after, etc.)
- Updates the "Events captured" blurb under the OpenCode setup
  section to reflect the full list (UserPromptSubmit and Stop were
  missing) and adds a note about the upstream MCP tool call limitation

https://claude.ai/code/session_01HpMgSN8uCgNypFeuDCo7x3

* feat(setup): add --cursor --global support

setup.sh --cursor always wrote to the project-level .cursor/hooks.json;
there was no way to target the global ~/.cursor/hooks.json without
manually editing the file.

Changes:
- Add CURSOR_GLOBAL variable; --global now sets it alongside
  CLAUDE_GLOBAL and OPENCODE_GLOBAL
- setup_cursor() branches on CURSOR_GLOBAL: global path is
  ~/.cursor/hooks.json, project path is unchanged
- Echo label updated to show (global: ...) or (project: ...) like
  the Claude and OpenCode functions already do
- Next-steps output notes when global hooks.json was written
- Usage comment and README Quick Start updated with --cursor --global

https://claude.ai/code/session_01HpMgSN8uCgNypFeuDCo7x3

* feat(opencode): add AfterFileEdit and PostToolUseFailure; fix table

Source-verified against sst/opencode (dev branch) to close the gaps
in the event coverage:

plugin/opencode.ts + examples/opencode-plugin.example.ts:
- file.edited → AfterFileEdit: properties contain exactly {file:string}
  (no session_id); emitted by write/edit/apply_patch tools
- tool.execute.after now inspects output.metadata.exit (the only
  failure signal for bash); non-zero exit emits PostToolUseFailure
  with exit_code and error="exit <n>", zero/absent emits PostToolUse

README Supported Events table:
- PostToolUseFailure: tool.execute.after (exit≠0)
- AfterFileEdit: file.edited
- Added footnotes explaining that bash/read/write/MCP/subagent tool
  calls all route through tool.execute.before/after (→ PreToolUse/
  PostToolUse with the relevant tool_name)
- Removed incorrect MCP limitation note — sst/opencode wraps MCP
  calls in the same tool.execute.before/after hooks
- Updated "Events captured" blurb to list all 8 emitted event types

tests/test_otel_hook.py: 6 new assertions (25 total in
TestOpenCodePluginPayloads) covering AfterFileEdit and
PostToolUseFailure IDE detection, canonical-name check, and
_get_event_name round-trip.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01HpMgSN8uCgNypFeuDCo7x3

* Update README.md

commit.message:
Fix KV cache context bleed in chat command

mlx_lm.server uses a trie-based prefix cache shared across ALL requests.
Single-turn messages share the '<|im_start|>user\n' token prefix, so a
new chat request could match a cached prefix from an unrelated earlier
request and inherit its context.

Fix: prepend a unique system message to every chat request so the token
sequence prefix is always different and never hits the shared cache.
Only affects 'mlx-server chat' — interactive REPL and direct API calls
are intentionally unchanged.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add 'chat' command for single-shot message sending

- mlx-server chat "<message>" — uses first loaded LLM automatically
- mlx-server chat --model <model> "<message>" — targets a specific model
- mlx-server chat --no-think "<message>" — disables reasoning mode (Qwen3 /no_think)
- Auto-starts gateway and auto-loads model if not already running
- Streams response to terminal with thinking shown in dimmed text

feat(service): add PreservePaths update support (#338)

* Support Service.psd1 for --content-root installs

Add support for Service.psd1 descriptors when installing services from a content-root (folder/archive/URL). The CLI now requires a Service.psd1 at the content root with mandatory Name, Description and Version (Script and ServiceLogPath are optional); Script defaults to server.ps1 when omitted. Parsing and validation were extended (new descriptor parsing helpers, normalization, and validation), ResolvedServiceScriptSource/ParsedCommand were enriched with descriptor metadata and option flags, and install logic now propagates resolved service name, version and effective log path (affecting bundle layout, preflight and platform install helpers). Also: forbid --name/--script when --content-root is used, update related error messages, adjust many method signatures accordingly, add CreateEmptyResolvedServiceScriptSource helper, and update unit tests and docs (including a tool-only Build-KestrunTool target and guidance) to reflect the new behavior.

* Add Service descriptor cmdlets and tests

Introduce Service.psd1 descriptor management: add New-KrServiceDescriptor, Get-KrServiceDescriptor, and Set-KrServiceDescriptor cmdlets and accompanying Pester tests. Update Kestrun.psd1 to export the new cmdlets.

- New-KrServiceDescriptor: creates a Service.psd1 with required Name/Description/Version and optional Script/ServiceLogPath, escapes single quotes, ensures directory exists, supports -Force and ShouldProcess, writes UTF-8 without BOM and returns parsed descriptor.
- Get-KrServiceDescriptor: reads and validates Service.psd1, ensures required keys and a valid Version, and returns a normalized ordered object.
- Set-KrServiceDescriptor: updates Description, Version, Script, and ServiceLogPath (Name is immutable), supports clearing Script/ServiceLogPath, validates mutually exclusive parameters, uses ShouldProcess, and returns the updated descriptor.
- Tests: add Pester tests covering create/read, update/clear behavior, and that Name is not an updatable parameter.

* Add .krpack package support & New-KrServicePackage

Introduce first-class .krpack service package support: add ServicePackageExtension, --package CLI option, and related validation/error messages; deprecate/rename older --content-root behavior and disallow script/name positional flags when installing from a package. Update service descriptor parsing to support FormatVersion 1.0 with EntryPoint and preserve legacy Script/Version handling. Add New-KrServicePackage PowerShell cmdlet to create .krpack archives (folder or script modes) and include tests for packaging and descriptor behavior. Also update help text and module exports accordingly.

* Add 'service update' and 'service info' commands

Implement service update and info functionality for kestrun tool. Adds new CommandMode entries (ServiceUpdate, ServiceInfo), parsing/validation and CLI options (--failback, --kestrun, --kestrun-module/--kestrun-manifest), and behavior to: resolve installed service bundles, produce an info JSON payload, update application/module/service-host from package or module manifest, create backups, and perform failback from the latest backup. Includes OS-specific service-state checks, bundled module version comparison (repository vs bundled), file-version-based service-host replacement, and cleanup of temporary content roots. Updates CLI help and README to document the new commands and options, adjusts parsing/validation to support the new modes, and adds tests/supporting PowerShell helper (Test-KrServiceDescriptorData.ps1). Also adds an example .krpack and renames/moves a couple of PowerShell script paths. Tests updated accordingly.

* Infer service Name from script and update defaults

Make New-KrServicePackage more user-friendly for ScriptPath mode: the Name parameter is no longer mandatory and, if omitted, defaults to the script filename (without extension). Description now defaults to the effective name when not provided, and the default output filename is changed to <Name>-<Version>.krpack. Updated descriptor escaping to use the effective name, added a Pester test to verify inferred name and default package path, added an example, and added '*.krpack' to .gitignore.

* Add --json output and list-all for service info

Enable structured JSON output and listing for `kestrun service info`. Changes include: add a JsonOutput flag to the service command records and parsing state, a CLI option `--json` (only for service info), and help/README updates. InfoService now supports either showing a single service (when --name is provided) or enumerating all installed services (when no name is given), and can emit either human-readable text or indented JSON. Added helpers: WriteServiceInfoHumanReadable and TryEnumerateInstalledServiceBundleRoots (chooses latest versioned bundle when needed). Service update logic was adjusted to allow package-only updates without --name by resolving the package descriptor for the service name. Service.psd1 Version is now validated to be compatible with System.Version. Tests were added/updated to cover parsing of service info without name, --json handling, package-only update parsing, and descriptor Version parsing. Minor parsing/validation tweaks and CLI usage text were also updated.

* Add backup snapshot support; rename script dir

Rename service bundle script folder constant from "script" to "application" and update callers/tests accordingly. Introduce ServiceBackupSnapshot record and GetServiceBackupSnapshots to enumerate and parse backup timestamp folders; include backup info in JSON output and human-readable service info. Simplify bundle resolution to use a non-versioned deployment root (remove selection of versioned subfolders) and update related docs/comments. Add System.Globalization using and adjust tests to cover backup snapshot behavior and the new application folder layout.

* Update Program.ServiceCommands.cs

* Update New-KrServiceDescriptor.ps1

* feat(service): add PreservePaths update support

* Add service CLI argument tests; update helper

Add multiple unit tests for service command argument parsing covering: content-root-dependent install options, failback rejecting content-root update options on update, unknown option handling across actions, and rejection of --package for certain service actions. New MemberData providers supply test cases for these scenarios. Also adjust InvokeGetServiceBackupSnapshots test helper to use Assert.IsType(..., exactMatch: false) for the enumerable result and remove an unnecessary null-forgiving operator when accessing snapshot properties. These changes improve CLI parsing coverage and relax a strict type assertion in the helper.

* Validate package versions and descriptor paths

Add robust package-version validation and tighten service descriptor path checks. Introduces TryValidateServicePackageVersionUpdate in the tool to parse package versions, warn when the installed descriptor version is missing, and error on non-increasing updates; updates call sites and unit tests. PowerShell descriptor validation now normalizes paths and uses GetRelativePath to ensure EntryPoint, Script and PreservePaths cannot escape the package root. Get-/Set-KrServiceDescriptor were updated to use the normalized descriptor and to surface clearer errors for missing or invalid Description/Version values. Update docs example to use -SourceFolder and add/adjust C# and PowerShell tests for the new behaviors.

* Refactor service update into modular helpers

Break out and reorganize the service update flow into smaller, testable Try* helper methods. UpdateService was refactored to validate options, resolve identity, resolve paths, apply package/module/host updates, and execute failback via dedicated helpers (TryValidateUpdateServiceCommand, TryResolveUpdateServiceIdentity, TryResolveServiceUpdatePaths, TryExecuteServiceFailback, TryApplyServicePackageUpdate, TryApplyServiceModuleUpdate, TryApplyDirectModuleReplacement, TryApplyServiceHostUpdate). Added ServiceUpdatePaths record to carry resolved paths and moved JSON summary/failback output to structured writers.

Also improved platform-specific service state checks by splitting Windows/Linux/macOS logic into TryEnsureWindowsServiceIsStopped/TryEnsureLinuxServiceIsStopped/TryEnsureMacServiceIsStopped and standardized error/exit code handling. Extracted archive script resolution into TryResolveServiceScriptFromExtractedArchiveContentRoot and consolidated service descriptor parsing with new helpers (TryReadNormalizedServiceDescriptorText, TryResolveServiceInstallDescriptor, TryResolveServiceDescriptorCoreFields, TryResolveServiceDescriptorEntryPointAndVersion, TryResolveOptionalServiceDescriptorVersion). Cleanup and backup/replace flows and error messages were clarified; temporary content cleanup remains handled in finally blocks.

* Add WhatIf/Confirm params to service cmdlet help

Add documentation for the standard PowerShell common parameters WhatIf and Confirm to the comment-based help of New-KrServiceDescriptor, New-KrServicePackage, and Set-KrServiceDescriptor. This change updates the .PARAMETER sections in the three service cmdlet files (src/PowerShell/Kestrun/Public/Service/*.ps1) to document preview and confirmation behavior; no runtime logic was modified.

* Support Service.psd1 format 1.0 (EntryPoint)

Introduce formal Service.psd1 format 1.0: require FormatVersion and EntryPoint (replacing legacy Script) and update docs/tooling examples. Validate and enforce content-root/package option rules in CLI parsing, and add dedicated content-root linked validation. Refactor service update flow in Program.ServiceCommands to modularize preparation, package/module/host update stages, and improve host update logic (resolve paths, backup/replace, version checks, chmod on Unix). Update PowerShell cmdlets (Get/New/Set-KrServiceDescriptor and internal descriptor validation) to emit and accept FormatVersion/EntryPoint, and adjust tests accordingly. Add guidance for .krpack lifecycle to tooling docs and align examples to use EntryPoint and content-root semantics.

* Update Program.ServiceParsing.cs

* Use Service.ps1 as default script

Change the default entry script from ./server.ps1 to ./Service.ps1 across the project. Updates include renaming/adding constants in the tool (RunDefaultScriptFileName, ServiceDefaultScriptFileName), updating Run and service resolution logic and help text, and updating docs and README examples. Tests adjusted to expect the new Service.psd1/EntryPoint message. This standardizes the default script name for both direct run and service scenarios.

* Add --raw output and structured service results

Add support for a --raw output mode and unified structured service operation results. Introduce ServiceControlResult and WriteServiceControlResult to emit table, JSON, or raw native command output; propagate a RawOutput flag through parsing and command plumbing. Update Windows/Linux/macOS start/stop/query implementations to return ServiceControlResult (capture stdout/stderr), add helpers to extract PIDs and detect already-stopped semantics, and adjust RunProcess calls to capture native output. Update CLI help and docs, add parsing validation (disallow --json with --raw), and add/adjust unit and PowerShell tests. Also include minor refactors (preflight/elevation handling and small cleanup).

* Refactor elevation relaunch; update docs

Refactor Windows elevation relaunch flow and improve error handling, output capture, and cleanup in Program.cs: extract helper methods (TryResolveElevationExecutablePath, WriteElevationWrapperScript, StartElevatedProcess, RelayElevatedOutput, WriteElevationCanceledMessage, WriteElevationFailureMessage), capture elevated process output via a temporary wrapper script, handle cancellation and failures explicitly, and ensure temporary files are removed.

Update docs/guides/production-nocontainer.md to focus on .krpack packaging: replace descriptor-driven content-root guidance with a detailed New-KrServicePackage section (two parameter sets, examples, checksum guidance), switch install examples to use --package, and tidy related notes about private package auth and certificate handling.

* Enhance service command management; add info option and improve Linux unit handling

* Update src/CSharp/Kestrun.Tool/README.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update service descriptor handling; rename Script to EntryPoint and improve property checks

* Docs: add service/daemon packaging and test fix

Add service/daemon packaging quick-start snippets and guidance across README, guides, docs index, and the Kestrun.Tool README. Rename docs/guides/production-nocontainer.md to production-service-daemon.md and update titles/links to reflect the Service/Daemon deployment model; add examples for New-KrServiceDescriptor and New-KrServicePackage (single-script packaging), descriptor verification, and upgrade workflow notes. Adjust SchedulerServiceTests to wait for and assert that LastRunAt is published after a runImmediate cron execution (increase wait timeout and check snapshot for timestamp).

* Update service lifecycle tests to improve output matching for query results

* Update Program.ServiceParsing.cs

* Update README.md

* Accept descriptor directory paths; enhance removal

PowerShell service descriptor cmdlets (New-/Get-/Set-KrServiceDescriptor) now accept either a Service.psd1 file path or a directory (auto-appends Service.psd1). New validation ensures EntryPoint is a relative path that resolves inside the descriptor directory and that the target file exists. Added comprehensive Pester tests for directory-path handling and EntryPoint error cases. C# tooling: wait for Windows service to reach STOPPED or be removed before deletion (added WaitForWindowsServiceToStopOrDisappear) and increase bundle delete retries on Windows; also loosened the PreservePaths validation to allow traversal (resolution is validated elsewhere). Docs updated and a sample PowerShell example removed; .gitignore updated to exclude Package-Sample.

- Add CHANGELOG.md documenting v0.1.0 features
- Helm: add copilotOrg, copilotPremiumPlan, ralphSnifferFile to values.yaml
- Helm: wire optional env vars with conditional templating
- Kustomize: add commented optional env vars (COPILOT_ORG, COPILOT_PREMIUM_PLAN, RALPH_SNIFFER_FILE)
- ArtifactHub: add ignore field for completeness

Fixes deployment gap where 3 optional env vars (COPILOT_ORG,
COPILOT_PREMIUM_PLAN, RALPH_SNIFFER_FILE) were documented in README
but missing from both Helm and Kustomize manifests.
